test_cases.title: Removed user receives access-denied on next request when no active session
preconditions: A project exists with at least one task and one content item
User A is a member of the project with verified access
User A does not have an active session at the time of removal
steps: Remove User A from the project via the appropriate admin/owner action
Authenticate as User A to establish a new session
Send a GET request to a project task resource (e.g., GET /projects/{projectId}/tasks/{taskId}) as User A
Send a GET request to a project content resource (e.g., GET /projects/{projectId}/content/{contentId}) as User A
test_cases.expected_result: Both requests return an HTTP 403 Forbidden (or equivalent access-denied) response; User A cannot retrieve any project task or content data.

test_cases.title: User with active session receives access-denied on next project resource request immediately after removal
preconditions: A project exists with at least one task and one content item
User A is a member of the project and has an active authenticated session
User A has successfully accessed a project resource within the current session to confirm access
steps: While User A's session remains active, remove User A from the project via the appropriate admin/owner action
Without User A logging out or refreshing their session, send a GET request to any project task resource using User A's existing session token
Record the HTTP status code and response body
test_cases.expected_result: The request returns an HTTP 403 Forbidden (or equivalent access-denied) response immediately, without any grace period; no project data is returned.

test_cases.title: Removed user with active session is redirected on next project resource request
preconditions: A project exists with at least one task
User A is a member of the project and has an active authenticated session
steps: Remove User A from the project while their session is active
Using User A's active session, send a request to a project resource URL
Observe the HTTP response code and any Location or redirect header
test_cases.expected_result: The response includes a redirect instruction (e.g., HTTP 302/303 with a Location header, or an access-denied page redirect) in addition to or following the access-denied response, directing User A away from the project resource.

test_cases.title: No grace period: access is revoked instantly, not after a delay
preconditions: A project exists with at least one task
User A is a member of the project and has an active session
steps: Record the exact timestamp T1 at which User A is removed from the project
Immediately (within 1 second of T1) send a request to a project task resource using User A's active session token
Record the HTTP status code and the timestamp T2 of the response
test_cases.expected_result: The access-denied response is received at T2 where T2 - T1 is less than any reasonable grace threshold (e.g., under 2 seconds); no project data is returned in the response.

test_cases.title: Non-member user who was never part of the project cannot access project resources
preconditions: A project exists with at least one task
User B has a valid system account but has never been added to the project
steps: Send a GET request to a project task resource belonging to the project
Record the HTTP status code and response body
test_cases.expected_result: The request returns an HTTP 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found response; no project data is exposed to User B.

test_cases.title: Remaining project members retain access after another user is removed
preconditions: A project exists with at least one task and one content item
User A and User B are both members of the project
User B has an active authenticated session
steps: Remove User A from the project
Using User B's active session, send a GET request to a project task resource
Send a GET request to a project content resource using User B's session
test_cases.expected_result: Both requests return HTTP 200 OK with the expected project task and content data; User B's access is unaffected by the removal of User A.

assumptions: The system uses token-based or session-based authentication; the access control check is performed server-side on every request, not solely at login/session-creation time.
The redirect destination for the removed user is not specified in the requirement; test cases verify only that a redirect occurs, not the specific destination URL.

An HTTP 403 Forbidden is the expected access-denied status code; 401 Unauthorized may also be acceptable depending on implementation, but the requirement implies the user is authenticated yet unauthorized.
The project removal action is performed by an authorized actor (e.g., project owner or administrator); the requirement does not describe who initiates the removal.
A grace period threshold of 2 seconds is used as a practical upper bound for the no-grace-period boundary test; the actual acceptable latency should be defined by system SLA.
notes: The requirement states the user 'shall be redirected' but does not specify the redirect target (e.g., dashboard, login page, error page). Test cases verify a redirect occurs but cannot assert the destination without further specification. The requirement does not clarify whether access-denied should be HTTP 403 or a different code, nor whether the redirect is a server-side HTTP redirect or a client-side navigation — this ambiguity is reflected in the test steps. It is also unspecified whether removal should invalidate the active session token entirely or merely deny access to project-scoped resources; this distinction could have broader security implications. Coverage of write operations (POST/PUT/DELETE on project resources) is not included because the requirement only references 'requests to a project resource' without distinguishing read vs. write, but separate test cases for write operations may be warranted.
